title: Nail strengthening oil
subtitle: A conditioning nail and cuticle oil blend with jojoba, vitamin E, and lemon essential oil
excerpt: A nail oil applied daily to the nail plate and surrounding cuticle keeps the nail flexible and less prone to splitting. This blend uses jojoba and fractionated coconut oil as the base, with vitamin E oil for oxidation stability and a small amount of lemon essential oil. Applied with a brush or dropper.
sourceNotes: Nail conditioning oils: Schoon, Nail Structure and Product Chemistry (2005). Jojoba oil as a nail treatment base: INCI Simmondsia chinensis (jojoba) seed oil; its molecular similarity to sebum aids penetration of the nail folds. Vitamin E (tocopherol) as antioxidant in oil blends: standard cosmetic formulation reference. Lemon EO at 1% as a leave-on product near the cuticle: IFRA limit for leave-on products is above 1% for most uses; caution with direct sun exposure to treated skin.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 15 ml nail oil pen or small bottle
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable preparation. Make a 30 ml batch and store in a small bottle; fill the nail pen as needed.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool dark place. The vitamin E oil extends the shelf life of the blend. Discard if the oil smells rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-free preparation. Nail oil contains no water and requires no preservative.

content.text: Nails that split, peel, or break frequently are often simply dry. A nail oil applied to the nail plate and cuticle daily adds flexibility by keeping the nail hydrated. This

blend uses jojoba as the primary carrier because its molecular weight and ester structure allow it to penetrate the nail folds more effectively than heavier oils. Fractionated coconut oil adds slip. Vitamin E oil acts as an antioxidant and extends the blend's shelf life. Lemon essential oil provides a clean scent; avoid applying to skin that will be immediately exposed to strong sunlight.

items.prepNote: 30% of blend; stays liquid in cold weather

content.text: Combine all ingredients in the dropper bottle. Swirl to mix. Label with the date. No heat is needed.

Apply 1 drop to each nail, working it into the nail plate and around the cuticle with a fingertip. The best time to apply is before bed, when the hands are not being washed. Daily application over several weeks improves nail flexibility noticeably. Massage the cuticle gently in circles to encourage absorption.
Apply a small amount to the inner forearm and wait 24 to 48 hours before using on the nails and cuticles. This is standard practice for any new cosmetic preparation.
Keep in a cool dark place. Keeps 12 months. Discard if the oil smells rancid. The vitamin E content extends the shelf life of the blend compared to a plain carrier oil.
